Most designers hand off and wait. I got tired of waiting. 8 years inside the unglamorous complexity of enterprise software — pricing engines, HR systems, supply chains. The kind of work where bad design costs real money. Now I'm learning to build what I design.

How I work

Design thinking
+ AI execution =
shipped products.

I use Claude Code and Cursor as build partners — not shortcuts. The goal is to own the full loop: understand the problem, design the solution, build it, ship it.

01

Problem first, tools second

AI doesn't replace understanding what's broken. Enterprise users work under pressure — that gap is still where real design happens.

02

AI as partner, not autocomplete

I'm learning why the code works, not just accepting what it produces. Blindly generated output I can't reason about is a liability.

03

Own the full loop

Design → build → ship → iterate. No handoff. No waiting. No decisions lost in translation between Figma and production.

Selected work

Case studies in outcomes

Price Guidance
$142
/seat · recommended
Confidence82%
Won 14/17 at this range
11% margin above floor
2 renewal risks — hold
Pricing Vendavo

Making price recommendations trustworthy enough to follow

Sales reps were overriding 73% of system price recommendations. Not because the prices were wrong — but because the system gave them no reason to trust it.

Problem Price guidance distrusted, consistently overridden
My Role Lead Designer, 0→1 research to ship
Key Decision Show reasoning, not just the recommendation
Outcome
41%reduction in price overrides
Research Enterprise UX Data viz
Read case study →
Onboarding Progress Day 3 of 14
✓Personal Info
✓IT Setup
→Compliance Modules
○Team Introduction
○Role Handbook
43%
HRTech Confidential client

Turning a compliance form into an onboarding experience

68% of new employees were abandoning mandatory onboarding before completion. The problem wasn't motivation — the system made people feel lost, not guided.

Problem 68% drop-off before compliance completion
My Role Lead Designer, end-to-end
Key Decision Treat it as a journey, not a form
Outcome
4.2×faster completion rate
Systems thinking User research Flows
Read case study →

About

Eight years of unglamorous work

I grew up in Kerala. There's a pace and a texture to life here that I carry into how I think — I don't like unnecessary noise, I like things that are considered, and I'm suspicious of anything that's trying too hard.

My day job has been enterprise UX. The kind of software that's complex and unsexy but teaches you a lot about how people actually use things under pressure. CRM, NGO, Finance, EdTech, HRTech, Supply Chain, and now Pricing at Vendavo.

Right now I'm learning to code. Properly. Not just copy-paste from AI — I'm trying to understand what I'm building. It's uncomfortable. I'm doing it anyway.

LinkedIn ↗ Dribbble ↗ Resume ↗

UI/UX Designer · CRM & Finance

2016

Figma didn't exist yet. Neither did this version of me.
